picks.reason: Jesús Luzardo owns a roughly three-quarter-run SIERA edge over Tyler Phillips, but the Marlins’ 126 wRC+ against lefties dwarfs the Phillies’ 84 wRC+ against righties, cancelling out the pitching advantage. Both bullpens carry fresh leverage arms and the park is neutral, leaving my estimated Phillies win probability within one point of the market.
The Blue Jays’ starting pitcher is listed as TBD. Without confirmed starter data, no edge can be estimated and the game is an automatic pass under the data-integrity rules.

Foster Griffin holds a roughly three-quarter-run SIERA advantage over Michael Wacha, but the platoon offensive splits are nearly identical and both bullpens enter with fresh leverage arms. My estimated Nationals win probability is only about 56 percent against a 55 percent market, falling short of the four-point gate.

Gerrit Cole carries a small-sample flag with only 22 innings of aggregate data, making his true-talent level unreliable. The competition prohibits three-unit plays on such starters, and the uncertainty compresses any edge below the betting threshold.

Kodai Senga’s 20-inning sample and lack of recent trend data make his skill level a guess, so the game is capped at no more than a lean. Even fading Senga, Brady Singer’s aggregate profile only creates a fractional edge that does not reach four points.

Grant Holmes owns a half-run SIERA edge over Adrian Houser and Atlanta’s offense is stronger, but Truist Park’s neutral factors and evenly rested bullpens keep the Braves’ win probability around 61 percent, just one point above the market. No actionable margin exists.

Robert Gasser is flagged as small-sample and his aggregate line is built on just 24 innings, so a three-unit play is off the board. Slade Cecconi’s superior track record and a friendly park only push my Cleveland estimate to about 43 percent against a 42 percent market, missing the gate.

Michael King and Andre Pallante grade out as virtual equals in aggregate run-prevention metrics, while St. Louis holds a 14-point wRC+ platoon edge at the plate. The slight offensive and home-field advantage keeps the Cardinals near market price with no discernible gap.

Edward Cabrera’s SIERA is almost a full run better than Ryan Feltner’s and the Cubs own the platoon advantage, but the combination still yields only a 2.8 percentage-point edge against a steep -186 market. That falls short of the four-point minimum required for a unit bet.

Zebby Matthews’ aggregate metrics are superior to Kumar Rocker’s, but his last-two-weeks SIERA has spiked toward 5.80, signaling degradation that wipes out the theoretical edge. The Twins’ win probability aligns too closely with the plus-money market to bet.

Hunter Brown’s small-sample tag removes any three-unit consideration, and while Framber Valdez has shown recent degradation, the Astros’ overall edge—pitching plus a 21-point wRC+ platoon advantage—only reaches about 2.5 points over the market. That sits below the betting gate.

Reid Detmers carries a full run of SIERA edge over Merrill Kelly, but the Diamondbacks’ offense sports a 118 wRC+ against lefties versus the Angels’ 98 against righties, cancelling most of the pitching value. My estimated Angels win probability is roughly 51 percent in a pick’em market, producing no gap.

Mitch Keller’s aggregate profile is superior to Jack Perkins’, yet Keller’s last-two-weeks degradation is severe and the Pittsburgh bullpen is only moderately rested. Sutter Health Park’s extreme run environment adds variance that further suppresses confidence, leaving the edge under two points.

Logan Gilbert’s aggregate SIERA is 1.58 runs lower than Brandon Young’s, and Seattle’s offense carries a measurable platoon edge in a pitcher-friendly park. After stripping out vig from the best available moneyline, Gilbert’s skill advantage translates to a 62.1 percent win probability against a 58.0 percent market, clearing the four-point gate for a one-unit play.

Drew Rasmussen owns a 1.14-run SIERA advantage over Justin Wrobleski, but the Dodgers’ lineup brings a 13-point wRC+ platoon edge and a loaded bullpen into Dodger Stadium. The Rays’ true win probability sits near 44 percent against a 42.5 percent market, failing to reach the four-point threshold.
